corners. For billions of years cells held on, 
fighting against the elements and each other, evolving in the process. Until one day they came 
together and made another jump in complexity, to plants, animals and fungi. First 
in the oceans, then on the land. Earth was now the stage of something grand, 
a complexity acceleration machine going at full speed. Millions of new species emerged and 
vanished. Life was beaten down over and over,
